reddit.summary: New accounts are heavily filtered. 10% rule for self-promotion. Domain bans are permanent and apply everywhere.
reddit.newAccountRestrictions: Posts auto-filtered until 100+ karma + 30+ days old. Rate limits on new accounts. Manual posting only — automation = domain ban.

rules.action: Posting multiple comments rapidly (6 in <5 min)
rules.note: New accounts have undisclosed rate limits. 6 comments in 3 minutes = shadow-ban risk. Max 1 comment per 10-15 min on new accounts. Space ALL posting activity.
Fully allowed. Best way to build karma fast.
Purpose-built for this. Link required. Engage with feedback.
Green light — must also engage with others' posts (reciprocity norm).
If someone asks 'how do I audit my landing page?' — fully fine to mention.
Official Reddit rule: 10% threshold for self-promotion is the line.
'I built PageScore which does X' — transparency is encouraged.

rules.action: Posting in r/webdev with heavy context
rules.note: Technical write-up showing HOW you built it = OK. 'Check out my tool' = removed.

rules.action: Feedback posts that are really ads
rules.note: Mods can tell. Works once, gets you banned if repeated.

rules.action: Same link posted across 5+ subreddits in 24h
rules.note: Spam filters flag cross-sub patterns. Space them out by days.

rules.action: Mobilizing Discord/Slack to upvote Reddit posts
rules.note: Vote manipulation = permanent site-wide ban + domain blacklist.
Reddit actively blocks automation. Manual only. API ToS prohibits bulk commercial posting.

rules.action: Buying votes or paying for promotion
rules.note: Explicit rule: 'You may never offer money or compensation to anyone to promote anything on reddit.'
Domain gets banned, not just the account. Nuclear option.

rules.action: Asking Twitter/Discord followers to upvote Reddit post
rules.note: Explicitly banned in Reddit wiki. Even mentioning it off-platform violates rules.

twitter.summary: Most organic activity is fine. Main risks are automation, coordinated amplification, and link suppression algorithm.
twitter.newAccountRestrictions: New accounts have 10 DM/day limit to non-followers. Less visible in search for ~30 days. Phone verification increasingly required.

rules.note: Fully allowed. Build-in-public content, results, case studies.

rules.action: Reply threads answering landing page questions
rules.note: Mentioning PageScore when genuinely relevant = fine.
'I analyzed 50 SaaS landing pages — here's what I found' = high-value, allowed.
Allowed with proper OAuth. Use Basic API tier ($100/mo) for write access.
Fine.

rules.action: Link in first tweet of thread
rules.note: Not banned but algorithm suppresses reach on tweets with external links. Put link in reply or last tweet.
Not banned but algorithmic suppression kicks in. Vary content.

rules.action: Tagging big accounts hoping for RT
rules.note: Not a ToS issue but often counterproductive. Build genuine relationship first.
Artificial amplification = account suspension.
Unsolicited bulk DMs = account lock/suspension.

rules.action: Automated bulk posting beyond API limits
rules.note: Free API = read-only. Basic tier = 3,000 tweets/month max.
Technically banned. Widely done but risky — account gets flagged.

devto.summary: Very founder-friendly. 'I built X' posts are culturally celebrated. Must be genuine content, not thin promo.
devto.newAccountRestrictions: New articles go through a brief review queue before full visibility. Rate limits on new accounts for following + publishing.

rules.note: DEV.to culture strongly supports this. Show the build, mention PageScore as context.
100% on-topic for dev audience. Our core domain.
Fine when genuinely relevant to the discussion.

rules.action: Cross-posting from blog with canonical tag
rules.note: Set canonical URL back to original. Explicitly allowed.
Must disclose AI assistance per Code of Conduct. Add a note at the bottom.

rules.action: Series of posts all pointing to PageScore
rules.note: OK if genuinely educational. Flagged if clearly a promotional funnel.

rules.action: Posts that are really just ads framed as tutorials
rules.note: Moderators actively watch for thin content that's just lead gen. Removed.

rules.action: Posts with no substantial content (just a link)
rules.note: Official rule: 'Posts must contain substantial content — not merely reference an external link.'

rules.action: Automated posting via API for promotional content
rules.note: Not explicitly allowed for promotional use. Manual only.

producthunt.summary: Legitimate launch platform. Rules are about authenticity of votes and engagement. Need 20+ supporters ready before launch.
producthunt.newAccountRestrictions: New accounts can comment/upvote but launches need an established hunter or account with history. Cold launches rarely succeed.

rules.note: Exactly what PH is for. Plan launch carefully — launch day team is critical.

rules.action: Asking maker friends to upvote on launch day
rules.note: Allowed if they're genuine users. Community support is expected.

rules.action: Having a hunter with 1000+ followers launch it
rules.note: Standard practice. DM potential hunters 2-4 weeks before.

rules.action: Replying to every comment on launch day
rules.note: Expected and boosts ranking. Personal, detailed responses.
Vote manipulation = product removal + account ban.
Not banned but practically fatal. First 2 hours = make or break.

rules.action: Upvote exchange groups ('I upvote yours, you upvote mine')
rules.note: Against rules. PH actively detects coordinated voting patterns.

hackernews.summary: Extremely strict. New accounts are invisible. Only technical, substantive content survives. One wrong move = death.
hackernews.newAccountRestrictions: New accounts cannot post for the first few days. Karma required for many actions. Posts from new accounts get heavily penalized in ranking.

rules.action: Show HN post (once account has history)
rules.note: Show HN is the legitimate way to launch on HN. Must be genuinely interesting technically.
Build karma slowly. Quality > quantity.

rules.action: Posting about the technical architecture of PageScore
rules.note: Must be technically interesting, not promotional. 'How I built an AI analyzer using X' format.
HN community is hostile to marketing language. 'Amazing tool' or 'check it out' = instant downvotes.

rules.action: Asking anyone to upvote your HN post
rules.note: Vote manipulation = account ban. HN is serious about this.
Will be ignored or flagged. Need established account first.

indiehackers.summary: Most founder-friendly platform. Built for sharing products and progress. Self-promotion is expected and welcomed.
indiehackers.newAccountRestrictions: Minimal restrictions for new accounts. Community is welcoming to genuine founders.

rules.action: Sharing PageScore in the products section
rules.note: Exactly what IH is for.
'Day 2 of building PageScore — here's what happened' = native content format.

rules.action: Asking for feedback on landing page
rules.note: Common and welcomed. Community gives genuine feedback.
Groups like 'Landing Pages' or 'SaaS' are perfect distribution channels.

rules.action: Spam posting or purely promotional content
rules.note: Community self-moderates. Repeated low-value posts get you ignored.
